title: Stolen Card Testing Across Multiple Shipping Addresses Within Rolling 7-Day Window
narrative: Fraudster has obtained valid card credentials and is conducting distributed CNP transactions across multiple shipping addresses to evade merchant-level fraud controls. Each individual transaction appears legitimate with correct billing information and reasonable amounts, but card-counter aggregations reveal the card is being shipped to far more distinct addresses in a 7-day window than any legitimate cardholder would use. This pattern exploits the fact that per-transaction fraud checks focus on billing verification and amount thresholds, while the card's cross-merchant footprint reveals systematic abuse. The fraudster intentionally keeps transaction amounts moderate and pacing normal to avoid velocity triggers, but the diversity of shipping contexts betrays the coordinated nature of the fraud operation.
indicators: C13 > 24 (p90), indicating card used with more than 24 distinct addresses in rolling window
C8 > 29 (p90), elevated card-to-address aggregation counter showing abnormal address diversity
C11 > 27 (p90), card-level counter flagging high distinct shipping destination count
C1 between 4 and 60 (p50-p90 range), overall card activity elevated but not extreme velocity
TransactionAmt between 50 and 200, amounts intentionally kept moderate to avoid triggers
D1 > 0 for majority of recent transactions, card paired with multiple different devices across address set
indicator_explanations: The card has been shipped to an unusually high number of different addresses recently
Card-to-address pairing shows the cardholder is using far more locations than normal
Aggregated shipping destinations for this card exceed typical cardholder patterns significantly
The card is being delivered to many distinct street addresses in a short timeframe
Overall card usage is elevated but paced to appear within acceptable transaction frequency
Transaction amounts stay in a moderate range that avoids automated fraud thresholds
The card appears with different devices across the various shipping addresses indicating multi-actor usage
summary: Card credentials are being exploited across multiple shipping addresses in a coordinated manner, with individual transactions appearing normal but card-level aggregations revealing systematic unauthorized use across diverse delivery locations.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely ship to more than 2-3 distinct addresses in a week. When card counters show 24+ distinct shipping addresses with moderate amounts and normal pacing, it indicates credential compromise where fraudsters are distributing orders across multiple drop addresses to monetize stolen card data while evading per-transaction fraud controls.
